summary: mongo_link.c calls WSACleanup during disconnection
description:
WSACleanup should only be called at exit, not on socket disconnect.

We should either register that to be called on exit or we should use a no-op Perl socket function to let Perl do WSAStartup for us and then let Perl do WSACleanup as well when the interpreter shuts down.

summary: request_id isn't thread safe
description:
Pull-Request on GitHub: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-perl-driver/pull/108]

This will be resolved in the v1 branch with the change to pure-Perl networking.

The question to consider is whether to patch it in the v0 series or not.  The PR fails, but it looks fairly trivial to fix.

summary: Die when encoding / decoding invalid UTF-8 strings
Update doesn't check for dots in field names
description:
This was reported as [rt.cpan.org #78461|https://rt.cpan.org/Public/Bug/Display.html?id=78461].

BSON serialization checks for dots in field names for inserts but not for updates.

MongoDB 2.6 will validate the document and return an error, but earlier versions will not and will store the invalid documents.

We should check whole-document replacements (but not update documents with operators).  {{find_and_modify}} is also likely buggy.

Implement Cluster/Server descriptions per CM spec
Allow count to work with query hints
Add driver standard connection URI options

Supports "slaveOk" and "connect=replicaSet"
Supports "slaveOk" and "connect=direct"
Supports "safe"

Supports "wtimeout"
Supports "fsync"
Supports connection timeout -- connectTimeoutMS
Supports timeout on socket connect on each send/receive -- socketTimeoutMS

summary: Reimplement server selection and read preferences
Failover of connection when primary replicate set server shutdown/stop does not work
description:
I connect using the following parameters and it works, it find what server is primary of the 2 node replicate set plus one arbiter node.

my $conn = MongoDB::Connection->new(host => 'mongodb://localhost:27017', find_master => 1);

I then do an update every second but if the primary Mongo node is stopped the fail over does not work but instead I get the following errors:

Odd number of elements in anonymous hash at /usr/lib/perl5/site_perl/5.8.8/Mouse/Meta/Method/Constructor.pm line 35.
Use of uninitialized value in subroutine entry at /usr/lib64/perl5/site_perl/5.8.8/x86_64-linux-thread-multi/MongoDB/Connection.pm line 460.

can't get db response, not connected at /usr/lib64/perl5/site_perl/5.8.8/x86_64-linux-thread-multi/MongoDB/Collection.pm line 528.
can't get db response, not connected at /usr/lib64/perl5/site_perl/5.8.8/x86_64-linux-thread-multi/MongoDB/Cursor.pm line 259.

Allow connecting to a replica set w/no primary

description:
This was originally reported as [rt.cpan.org #63955|https://rt.cpan.org/Ticket/Display.html?id=63955].  See that ticket for details.

It might have been fixed by https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-perl-driver/commit/d64e8f2666b21303274d24ef6408442dd2901434 around the same time.

summary: Driver throwing exceptions under Dancer/Starman
description:
Requests to a web application sometimes fail due to MongoDB Perl driver throwing exceptions. Exception messages include:

	▪	invalid header received at /usr/local/lib/perl/5.14.2/MongoDB/Cursor.pm line 160.
	▪	missed the response we wanted, please try again at /usr/local/lib/perl/5.14.2/MongoDB/Cursor.pm line 160.
	▪	can't get db response, not connected at /usr/local/lib/perl/5.14.2/MongoDB/Cursor.pm line 160.

Me feeling is, that is happens if the load (requests/s) to the application is above average. I don't have a "safe" way to reproduce but i think 1 of 2000 requests throws a message like that. There are 5-50 requests per second so that the problem occurs on a regular basis.

The application is running as a Dancer app inside Starman server on a Ubuntu 12.04.3 LTS 64bit machine which also hosts the MongoDB (connections are made to localhost).

The database object is created when the worker is spawned and is then kept persistent.

The interesting point is, that i'm running 0.702.2 (which is not in the list of versions in JIRA) of the MongoDB perl driver that should have fixed these issues according to PERL-162.

summary: Implement support for NEAREST read preference
description: NEAREST was never implemented.  Once cluster monitoring is implemented (thus tracking round-trip-times for servers), then NEAREST support should be added.

When I replace MongoDB:Connection with MongoDB::MongoClient the Perl script exit with the following status during the invocation of the script:

Can't locate object method "_init_conn" via package "MongoDB::MongoClient" at /usr/local/lib64/perl5/MongoDB/MongoClient.pm line 349

summary: Provide way to pass IO::Socket::SSL arguments
description: Users may want to customize IO::Socket::SSL.  We need to provide a way to pass through options.  Possibly, if {{ssl}} is a hash reference, we pass it through?  That would avoid another attribute.

summary: Add support for read preference options in the connection string
description:
The Perl driver needs to support these connection string options:

* readPreference
* readPreferenceTags

summary: use http-style SSL hostname verification
description: The MongoDB server uses 'http' style hostname verification.  The Perl driver should do the same.

summary: The result is wrong when mulit-process query concurrently
Can't locate object method "new" via package "Config::AutoConf" at inc/Module/Install/PRIVATE/Mongo.pm line 126.
description:
I have the requisite perl modules installed:

$ dlocate Config/AutoConf.pm Config/Tiny.pm
libconfig-autoconf-perl: /usr/share/perl5/Config/AutoConf.pm
libconfig-tiny-perl: /usr/share/perl5/Config/Tiny.pm

Yet when I type "perl Makefile.PL", I get an error:

$ perl Makefile.PL 
Can't locate object method "new" via package "Config::AutoConf" at inc/Module/Install/PRIVATE/Mongo.pm line 126.

I see that Mongo.pm loads Config::AutoConf before calling it:

$ grep Config::AutoConf inc/Module/Install/PRIVATE/Mongo.pm
use Config::AutoConf;
    my $ca = Config::AutoConf->new;
        my $ca = Config::AutoConf->new;

(... further experimentation ...)

Upgrading Config::AutoConf from the Ubuntu-supplied version 0.16 to the CPAN latest 0.305 seems to have solved the problem.

Perhaps MongoDB should require some minimum version of Config::AutoConf?

summary: Fix tagset logic in read preference application
description: Only the first tagset to match any secondary should be used.

summary: Use JSON::MaybeXS instead of JSON
description: JSON::MaybeXS provides better support for JSON across multiple backends, including pure-perl.

summary: Inserting DateTime->from_epoch(epoch => 0) fails
Revise server selection implementation
description: Changes to the server selection spec need to be mirrored in the driver.

summary: Support setting write concern at the database and collection level
Allow both multiple => 1 and mulit => 1 in the update options
description:
Most documentation and the mongodb shell use {multi:1} as an option to updates.  Cool.  Go code some perl and add {multi=>1} to my options.  No errors, but wait, only one item was changed.  Hours running down possible problems ensue, finally, out of desperation, I check the perldoc for MongoDB::Collection and see that I actually need {multiple=>1}.  

Consistency would really be cool.  You could support both and you won't break existing code.  Alternatively, you could error out or otherwise warn someone that "multi" != "multiple" in the perl driver.

summary: t/database.t fails on driver version 0.707.0.0 when tests run against 2.8.0 mongod
description:
Hey guys,

I noticed the Perl-driver test t/database.t fails on version 0.707.0.0 when a 2.8.x Mongod is running during 'make test'.

It seems the output for a non-existant db.runCommand changed in 2.8, see below:

=== Mongo 2.6.x: ===
"timtest1:PRIMARY> db.version()
2.6.4
timtest1:PRIMARY> db.runCommand({ "foo" : "bar"})
{
        "ok" : 0,
        "errmsg" : "no such cmd: foo",
        "code" : 59,
        "bad cmd" : {
                "foo" : "bar"
        }
}"

=== Mongo 2.8.x ===
"> db.version()
2.8.0-rc1
> db.runCommand({ foo : "bar"})
{
        "ok" : 0,
        "errmsg" : "no such command: foo",
        "code" : 59,
        "bad cmd" : {
                "foo" : "bar"
        }
}"

The t/database.t test fails on line 72 with the following:

"#   Failed test 'error from non-existent command'
#   at t/database.t line 72.
#                   'no such command: foo'
#     doesn't match '(?-xism:no such cmd|unrecognized command)'
# Looks like you failed 1 test of 15.
t/database.t ........... "

The reason for this failure is the change in output in 2.8.x to "no such command:" instead of "no such cmd:". This should be easy to fix :).

Apologies if this is a duplicate.

Cheers,

Tim

summary: Aggregation cursors don't prevent illegal methods
description: Some methods shouldn't be called on aggregation cursors.  Cursor objects need a way to identify aggregation cursors and error on illegal methods.

summary: aggregate broken for db names with non alphanumeric characters
Investigate safe_mode default interfering with w=1 default
description: Per report from {{_sri}} on IRC, figure out why the {{batch_insert}} logic still has a deprecated safe_mode check which I thought I removed.

summary: MongoClient appends :27107 when host is given as server:port
description: We should either detect that a port is already there or else throw an error that the host must either be just a server or be a full connection URI

summary: Use a better Query data model
description:
The query class should separately model "criteria", "projections", "modifiers" and other query options that go into the wire protocol.

That would simplify the Cursor class -- it would merely hold a Query object and a lazily generate a QueryResult object.  Almost everything else would be delegated methods.

It would also simplify the query methods in the client.  They would take Query objects and return QueryResult objects, rather than consuming a positional list of parameters, some of which are often undefined.

summary: Rationalize cursor batch size control
description:
Currently, there is no public option to control batch size (though limit affect batch size).  We should make batch size public and have actual batch size be the minimum of batch size and limit.

Aggregation cursors also need to support a batch size option.  Python uses a "Command Cursor" class distinct from the regular cursor.  This might be a better approach than the how we fake up an already-running cursor object to return.

summary: Don't use $query when querying against the $cmd collection.
Implement AwaitData
description: Two new query options (AwaitData and Exhaust) and all new response flags (CursorNotFound, QueryFailure, and AwaitCapable) have been added to the Mongo wire protocol (http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Mongo+Wire+Protocol). The driver should handle the response flags appropriately (eg. CursorNotFound should throw exception rather than silently end the query results). The driver may implement the new query options if desired.

summary: index creation should default to w=1 without an explicit write concern
description: Currently, index creation uses the implicit write concern of the client.  That setting isn't necessarily appropriate for index creation.

summary: Incorrect documentation of cursor snapshot feature
description: Per https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-14703, setting snapshot on a cursor doesn't prevent it from omitting documents in the resultset that are updated during the lifetime of the query, they *only* prevent dups. As such, the 'or objects missed' language needs to be changed. The parenthetical '(if an object is new during the query, or deleted during the query, it may or may not be returned, even with snapshot mode)' should become a first-class sentence something like 'If an object is created, deleted, or updated during the lifetime of a query, it may or may not be returned, even with snapshot [set to true (or lang-specific equivalent)].'

summary: ensure_index on arrayref dies if first index type is not numeric
description:
The {{ensure_index}} method has various checks for valid inputs.  One of those checks is this:

{code}
       (ref $keys eq 'ARRAY' &&
         ($#$keys == 0 || $#$keys >= 1 && !($keys->[1] =~ /-?1/))) 
{code}

This dies if the first type is a non-numeric index type.

I think the other, related validation clauses are inconsistent in other ways and they all need to be rationalized.

(Initially reported as [Meerkat #6|https://github.com/dagolden/Meerkat/issues/6])

summary: Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes commands
Fix TCP_DELAY
mongo_connect and Mongo should fail if can't connect
description:
connect should make sure the connection can be open and return an error

new Mongo() should fail in the php appropriate way.

summary: memory leak with mongo_insert
description:
behaviour:

if you do several inserts in a row the php process consumes more and more memory,
this happens step wise, however the steps are in no regular interval,
meaning every x inserts the process needs more memory, 
where x in our tests was between 400 and 900 

Script for reproduction:
<?php
$con = mongo_connect("localhost","","",false,false,false); // get connection
$arr = array("test" => "1, 2, 3"); // simple array with data

for($i = 1; $i < 2000000; $i++) // lets do it for 2 million times 
{
  mongo_insert($con, "foo.test", $arr);
  echo "memory usage: " . memory_get_usage(true) ."\n"; // print out current memory usage
}
?>


the resulting output of a run is attached as a file.

summary: Paired Connections
description:
Hello!

I have set up 2 different computers with mongodb installed and running
with no problems - the replica pair way. They both distinguish between
who is master and who is slave and writing to master works perfect.
(http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Replica+Pairs)

The problem arises with the PHP driver which I have to use in order to
access the database. While the database master IP can change I'm
wondering how to use PHP driver in order to connect to the right
master at all times.

I thought this would work, but it doesn't output any errors of any
kind and it doesn't do anything. I can't see any output or errors in
console (or PHP error log) where mongodb is running.

Everything is set up on OS X 10.5 Leopard Servers.

The code that I use for connecting:
       $m = new Mongo( "IP1:27017,IP2:27017");
       $m->pairConnect();

When I try to connect directly to master mongodb server driver works
OK.

Thank you for helping.

summary: check on create collection bug
description: make sure http://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PYTHON-12 isn't happening
